---
title: "Setup"
---

Please complete setup **before** your workshop. If you get stuck, come to the optional
"install party" or post in the workshop chat — don't suffer in silence.

::: {.callout-important}
## The fastest path: cloud environment
Every ForgeLab workshop offers a one-click cloud environment (GitHub Codespaces) with
everything preinstalled. If your laptop is locked down or setup fails, use the cloud
fallback — you lose nothing. Your instructor will share the link.
:::

## 1. A terminal / shell

::: {.panel-tabset}
### Linux
You already have a terminal (e.g. GNOME Terminal). You're set.

### macOS
Use the built-in **Terminal** app, or install [iTerm2](https://iterm2.com/).

### Windows
Install [Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL2)](https://learn.microsoft.com/windows/wsl/install):
```powershell
wsl --install
```
Then use the **Ubuntu** terminal for all workshop commands.
:::

## 2. Git

Verify or install:

```bash
git --version    # expect git version 2.x or newer
```

If missing: Linux `sudo apt install git`, macOS `brew install git` (or Xcode CLT),
Windows use the WSL Ubuntu instructions above. Then set your identity:

```bash
git config --global user.name  "Your Name"
git config --global user.email "you@example.com"
```

## 3. Python 3.10+

```bash
python3 --version
```

We recommend [Miniforge](https://github.com/conda-forge/miniforge) or your system Python
with a virtual environment:

```bash
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate          # Windows (WSL) / Linux / macOS
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

## 4. Robotics track only: ROS 2

The Robotics & Autonomy track uses **ROS 2 (Jazzy)**. The simplest cross-platform option is
the official Docker image — no system-wide install required:

```bash
docker pull ros:jazzy
docker run -it --rm ros:jazzy bash
```

Native install instructions: <https://docs.ros.org/en/jazzy/Installation.html>.
Your instructor will confirm which option the workshop uses.

## 5. A GitHub account

Create one free at <https://github.com/join> if you don't have one — we use it for
collaboration, pull requests, and the cloud environment.

## Check your setup

```bash
git --version && python3 --version && echo "Core setup OK"
```

If that prints versions and `Core setup OK`, you're ready. 🎉
